title: sagraea pilosa
intro: Sagraea pilosa, also known as the hairy sagraea, is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Sagraea pilosa include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, Sagraea pilosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used to make herbal teas or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
